title: 5 Enrolling in the AFT Service
body_text:
5	Enrolling in the AFT Service
5.1	Overview of Enrolling in the AFT Service
It generally takes two weeks from the date Central 1 receives the required documents to set up AFT service for a new financial institution or business member Originator.
Enrollment to the AFT Service includes the following steps:
1.	Decide on AFT Service Type and File Upload or Release Method
The financial institution or business member Originator must choose a method of generating and submitting AFT Transactions. Originators either input AFT Transactions directly on
PaymentStream
AFT or on alternate software to create the transactions.
â
Originating AFT via
PaymentStream
AFT
â See the Originating AFT via
PaymentStream
AFT Part
â
Originating AFT via File Transmission
(BC and Atlantic regions only)
â Originators that use in-house or third-party software to generate AFT Transactions can upload AFT files to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. Once an AFT file is uploaded, it is validated and delivered to Central 1âs point of exchange with other financial institutions. See the Originating AFT via File Transmission (BC) Part for more information.
Note:	BC and Atlantic region Originators that upload AFT files to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange must apply for access if it is not already set up. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information.
The following table summarizes the options available for generating AFT Transactions and for uploading or releasing AFT files to Central 1.
AFT Service Option
Options within the Service
PaymentStream
AFT
Create AFT Transactions directly on
PaymentStream
AFT and manually release AFT files.
Create the AFT Transactions directly on
PaymentStream
AFT and release AFT transactions automatically.
Create the AFT Transactions using third-party or in-house software and upload the AFT file to
PaymentStream
AFT.
File Transmission to the File and Report Exchange (BC and Atlantic region only)
Create the AFT Transactions using third party or in-house software. Upload the AFT file to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
2.	Decide Which Party Inputs the AFT Transactions (for Business Member Originators Only)
The financial institution and business member must decide which party will input the AFT Transactions if the Originator is a business member. The financial institution will always input AFT Transactions for individual members (e.g., mortgage or loan PADs).
If the financial institution inputs AFT Transactions on behalf of a business member, the following decisions must be considered:
â
How the business member will provide the financial institution with transaction updates and how much Lead Time is required
â
If the business member requires any reports to facilitate account reconciliation and how these will be distributed
â
Who and how the business member will be contacted regarding rejected and returned transactions
â
How the business member will communicate account corrections
3.	Determine the Type of AFT Transactions the Originator Will Issue
Determine if the Originator will issue PADs and/or Direct Deposits. If the Originator will issue PADs, determine the PAD type (Business PADs, Cash Management PADs, Funds Transfer PADs, or Personal PADs). Business member Originators cannot issue Funds Transfer PADs. See Chapter 2, Terminology and Frequently Asked Questions, for a definition of each PAD type.
The type of AFT Transactions that the Originator issues affects which forms and agreements are required.
4.	Complete the Appropriate Service Agreement
If the Originator is a business member, the financial institution and the business member must sign one of the following agreements:
Note:	Business members that originate PADs can only issue Personal PADs, Business PADs, or Cash Management PADs. They cannot issue Funds Transfer PADs.
â
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904
â Complete this agreement if the business member Originator will issue Personal PADs or Business PADs.
â
Letter of Undertaking for
Cash Management PADs, Form 2007
â Complete this agreement if the business member Originator will issue Cash Management PADs.
â
Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043
â Complete this agreement if the business member will issue Direct Deposits.
5.	Advise the Originator to Obtain Payorâs PAD Agreements from Each Payor (for PADs Only)
The Originator, regardless of whether it is the financial institution or a business member, must obtain a Payorâs PAD Agreement from each Payor.
It is recommended that Originators use the Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696. If the business member chooses to use its own version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement, the financial institution must review it for CPA compliance.
A Payorâs PAD Agreement is required for Business PADs, Funds Transfer PADs, and Personal PADs, but it is not required for Cash Management PADs.
6.	Obtain a Voided Cheque and Direct Deposit Application from the Payee (for Direct Deposits Only)
If a business member will issue Direct Deposits (e.g., for payroll), it is recommended that each Payee provide the business member Originator with a voided cheque on the account to be credited. Completion of the Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000, is optional and is at the discretion of the Originator.
If an individual (non-business) member wants to send Direct Deposits to an account at another financial institution, it is recommended that the financial institution obtain a voided cheque on the account to be credited and the Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Members, Form 5966, from the member.
7.	Complete the Application for AFT Service and the User Request Forms
PaymentStream
AFT
Financial institutions complete the PaymentStream AFT Service Application, Form 5329, for all new AFT Originators regardless of which party inputs the transactions, which AFT service is used, or which type of AFT Transactions are issued.
8.
Obtain a User ID and a Temporary Password
For new Client Centre users, a CPS Admin Security Officer will create a profile in User Management and communicate a temporary password in a secure manner. For existing Client Centre users, assign the individual access to
PaymentStream
AFT and define permissions in User Management. See the User Management Part for information on creating or modifying Client Centre users and defining
PaymentStream
AFT user permissions.
9.
Conduct a Test Run
In
PaymentStream
AFT, Originators can test their file directly on the application to confirm the validity of the AFT file. The system automatically validates a test file when it is uploaded and provides error messages, warnings, or notices to help the user rectify any issues. For procedures on uploading a test file in
PaymentStream
AFT, see the
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for File Upload, Form 5600.
10.	Advise Business Member Originators of Their Obligations under CPA Rule H1 (for Business Member PAD Originators Only)
The financial institution is required to provide business member PAD Originators with full information on CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits (PADs).
11.	Determine How to Obtain AFT Settlement Reports (for Business Member Originators Only)
All business member Originators download their AFT settlement reports from
PaymentStream
AFT. If the financial institution inputs transactions on behalf of a business member Originator, the parties decide how and when the AFT settlement reports will be provided to the business member.
Financial institution Originators download their AFT settlement reports from Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. See the AFT Reports Part for more information.
Related Information
Application for AFT Service via FTP,
Form 1378
Payorâs PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans,
Form 1904
Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors,
Form 2000
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs,
Form 2007
Direct Deposit Agreement,
Form 2043
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application,
Form 5329
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for Automatic Release,
Form 5598
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for Manual Release,
Form 5599
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for File Upload,
Form 5600
Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Member Payors,
Form 5966
5.2	Summary of AFT Agreements and Application Forms
See the following table for a summary of the agreements and application forms used to enroll Originators or individual members in the AFT Service.
Agreement or Form Name
Description
Type
Document Completed by
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904
This agreement is between the financial institution and the business member Originator. It is required if the business member Originator will issue Personal PADs or Business PADs.
Personal PADs or Business PADs
Financial institution and business member Originator
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007
This agreement is between the financial institution and the business member Originator. It is required if the business member will issue Cash Management PADs.
Cash Management PADs
Financial institution and business member Originator
Application for AFT Service via FTP, Form 1378
This form is for BC and Atlantic regions only. It is used by new Originators to apply for AFT service (File Transmission via FTP).
Note: If the financial institution already inputs AFT Transactions for individual members, an Originator ID exists. It is not necessary to complete this form each time a member requests a new AFT Transaction (e.g., a mortgage payment).
PADs and Direct Deposits
Financial institution
Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696
This agreement is made between the Originator (Payee) and the Payor.
If the financial institution issues PADs for individual members (e.g., for loan or mortgage payments) it must obtain the agreement from the member (Payor).
If the Originator is a business member, the business member must obtain the agreement from its client (the Payor).
Personal PADs, Business PADs, and Funds Transfer PADs
The Payee and the Payor. (The Payee can be the financial institution or a business member depending on who the Originator is.)
FTP User Request, Form 2010
Required by Originators that upload AFT files via FTP. Complete one form for each user that requires access to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on setting up users with access to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange.
PADs and Direct Deposits
Financial institution
Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043
This agreement is between the financial institution and the business member. It is required if the business member Originator will issue Direct Deposits.
Direct Deposits
Financial institution and business member Originator
Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000 (optional)
Use this form if a business member Originator is the Payor (e.g., for payroll deposits). This form provides the business member Originator with information on the account to be credited. It is recommended that the customer (Payee) provide the Originator with a voided cheque.
Direct Deposits
Business member Originator (Payor) and its customer (Payee)
Direct Deposit Application â Non-Business Member Payors, Form 5966 (optional)
Use this form if a non-business member wants to send Direct Deposits to an account at another financial institution (e.g., to send monthly payments to a family member). This form provides the financial institution with information on the account to be credited and authorization to debit the memberâs account. Obtain a voided cheque from the Payee.
Direct Deposits
The Payee and the Payor (the member). The Payee may or may not be the same individual as the Payor. The completed document is provided to the financial institution.
Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member, Form 3701
This agreement highlights the use of
PaymentStream
AFT software and the obligations of business members that use the software. It also provides additional terms for protection and liability. Financial institutions can review their existing business service/relationship agreements to ensure they have adequate coverage. Financial institutions have the option to use this form for additional coverage.
PADs and Direct Deposits
Financial institution and business member Originator
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329
Used by Originators to apply for, change, or cancel
PaymentStream
AFT service.
Also used by Originators to apply for the AFT Re-presentment service.
Note: If the financial institution already inputs AFT Transactions for individual members, an Originator ID exists. It is not necessary to complete this form each time a member requests a new AFT Transaction (e.g., a mortgage payment).
AFT service application
Financial institution
File Limit Change Request AFT Service via FTP, Form 1641
This form is for BC and Atlantic regions only. Use this form to increase an Originatorâs file Limits.
Limit Change
Business member Originator
AFT Dormant Period Request, Form 2044
Use this form for clients that request a dormant period for an AFT transaction.
Dormant Request
Financial institution members or business member clients
Direct Deposit Request, Form 6171
Use this form to request the set-up of Direct Deposits from an Organization to a memberâs account. The member provides this form to the organization that originates the deposits (e.g., the employer).
Direct Deposit
Financial institution members
Related Information
Application for AFT Service via FTP,
Form 1378
File Limit Change Request AFT Service via FTP,
Form 1641
Payorâs PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans,
Form 1904
Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors,
Form 2000
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs,
Form 2007
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
Direct Deposit Agreement,
Form 2043
AFT Dormant Period Request,
Form 2044
Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member,
Form 3701
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application,
Form 5329
Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Member Payors,
Form 5966
Direct Deposit Request,
Form 6171
5.3	Setting up Originators for AFT Re-presentment
AFT Re-presentment is an optional service for
PaymentStream
AFT Originators. This service can be requested when a new AFT Originator is set up for
PaymentStream
AFT or when an existing
PaymentStream
AFT Originator ID wants to add the service. AFT Re-presentment allows a
PaymentStream
AFT Originator to have a previously returned AFT debit resubmitted automatically on a one-time basis. This eliminates the need for the AFT Originator to create an additional one-time only AFT debit for resubmission back to the destination financial institution.
Debit returns that are eligible for AFT Re-presentment are limited to the following two AFT return reason codes:
901 NSF (Not Sufficient Funds)
908 Funds Not Cleared
The following CPA Rules apply to AFT Re-presentment:
AFT debits are eligible for Re-presentment on a one-time only basis within 30 days following the return of the original AFT debit transaction.
Note: 	Although Payments Canada allows for Re-presentment to occur within 30 days following the return of the original AFT debit, Central 1âs AFT Re-presentment service has a limitation of eight business days.
Since the payor must have an authorized PAD agreement in place, the return of the original AFT debit can be re-presented as set out in CPA Rule F1.
An AFT re-presented debit is for the exact same value as the original AFT debit and no additional service fees are added to the re-presented debit.
Timing of AFT Re-presentment
The timing of when AFT debit returns are re-presented by Central 1 depends on the number of business days a financial institution requests for the auto Re-presentment and when the AFT return is received at Central 1.
The number of days to activate the Re-presentment activity can be requested in a range of zero to eight business days. Examples are defined as follows:
â0 business daysâ
â Upon receipt of the returned AFT debit, an eligible AFT re-presented debit will be created within the same day and prepared for delivery back to the destination financial institution. The only exception is if the debit is received after 9:00 pm ET, in which case the re-presented debit is delivered the following day.
â1 business dayâ
â Upon receipt of the returned AFT debit, an eligible AFT re-presented debit will be created one business day later and prepared for delivery back to the destination financial institution.
â8 business daysâ
â The maximum allowable Re-presentment time frame. Upon receipt of the returned AFT debit, an eligible AFT re-presented debit will be created eight business days later and prepared for delivery back to the destination financial institution.
Settlement Information for AFT Re-presentment
Generally, the AFT Re-presentment Offset CR is directed to the original AFT debitâs Account Number for Returns. To assist with variances on how financial institutions may reconcile their AFT business membersâ accounts, financial institutions can request that the offsetting credit transactions be directed to an alternate account, such as a holding account.
5.4	Indicating AFT Re-presentment Preferences
Complete
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329, for new Originator IDs with AFT Re-presentment or to add AFT Re-presentment to existing Originator IDs. See Figure 1. Completion of the form is self-explanatory but see procedures below for indicating AFT Re-presentment preferences.
Figure 1: PaymentStream AFT Service Application, Form 5329
Requesting a New Originator ID with AFT Re-presentment
â
Select
New OID
. See Figure 2.
â¡
Select
Yes
for AFT Re-presentment.
â¢
Enter a value from â0â to â8â to indicate the number of business days for the automatic Re-presentment.
â£
Enter a note in the Comments field to indicate the start date, âAFT Representment start date mm/dd/yyyy.â The service will be activated as of this date.
Figure 2: Requesting AFT Re-presentment for New Originator IDs
â¤
Provide the transit, route, and account number for the AFT Originatorâs Settlement Account. The same account can be used for the AFT Re-presentment Offset CR. See Figure 3.
â¥
Enter the account number for the AFT Offset CR if the financial institution is using a different account for the AFT Offset CR. Do not fill in these fields if the Offset CR is to be directed to the original AFT debit Account Number for Returns. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Indicating Settlement Account Information for New Originator IDs with AFT Re-presentment
Requesting Re-presentment for an Existing Originator ID
â
Select
Change OID
and provide the existing OID. See Figure 4.
â¡
Select
Yes
for AFT Re-presentment.
â¢
Enter a value from â0â to â8â to indicate the number of business days for the automatic Re-presentment.
â£
Enter a note in the Comments field to indicate the start date, âAFT Re-presentment start date mm/dd/yyyy for existing AFT OID.â The service will be activated as of this date.
Figure 4: Requesting AFT Re-presentment for Existing Originator IDs
â¤
Provide the transit, route, and account number for the AFT Originatorâs Settlement Account. The same account can be used for the AFT Re-presentment Offset CR. See Figure 5.
â¥
Enter the account number for the AFT Offset CR if the financial institution is using a different account for the AFT Offset CR. Do not fill in these fields if the Offset CR is to be directed to the original AFT debit Account Number for Returns. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Indicating Settlement Account Information for Existing Originator IDs with AFT Re-presentment
Upon receipt of the
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329, Central 1 sets up the AFT Re-presentment Originator ID and makes the necessary modifications to the existing AFT Originator ID within Central1âs internal AFT processing system. Confirmation of the request, the AFT Re-presentment start date, and any other updates are communicated to the financial institution through ServiceNow.
5.5	Enrolling in AFT Service for PADs
This Section describes how to set up Originators with AFT Service for PADs.
Adding Financial Institution Originator IDs
Most financial institutions are already set up as Originators to issue PADs for individual members (e.g., for mortgage and loan payments). To set up another Originator ID for the financial institution, complete
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329, and forward to Central 1.
Adding New Business Member Originator IDs
Proceed as follows to set up a new business member Originator with AFT Service for PADs:
Note:	Business members can only issue Personal PADs, Business PADs, or Cash Management PADs. They cannot issue Funds Transfer PADs.
Option 1: Complete the Letter of Undertaking for
Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904 (for Personal PADs and Business PADs)
â CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, requires the financial institution and its business member (the Payee) to sign a Letter of Undertaking. If the business member will issue Personal PADs or Business PADs, complete the Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904. Ensure that you enter the number of days required to terminate the agreement in Article 7, Termination. A shorter notice period allows the financial institution to better mitigate risks. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904
The Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans , Form 1904, complies with CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, but it doesnât constitute legal advice. It is recommended that financial institutions use this version of the agreement. If your financial institution chooses to alter the agreement, all changes must be legally reviewed by your financial institutionâs legal counsel for CPA compliance.
Note:	Central 1 does not require a copy of the signed Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904.
Note:	If the business member has a third party generate AFT Transactions on its behalf, it should enter into a separate agreement with the third party. The Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904, only addresses the AFT arrangement between the financial institution and the business member.
Option 2: Complete the Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007
(for Cash Management PADs)
â CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, requires the financial institution and its business member (the Payee) to sign a Letter of Undertaking. If the business member will issue Cash Management PADs, complete the Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007. Ensure that you enter the number of days required to terminate the agreement in Article 7, Termination. A shorter notice period allows the financial institution to better mitigate risks. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007
The Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007, complies with CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits, but it doesnât constitute legal advice. It is recommended that financial institutions use this version of the agreement. If your financial institution chooses to alter the agreement, all changes must be legally reviewed by your financial institutionâs legal counsel for CPA compliance.
Note:	Cash management PADs do not require the Payor to complete the Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696.
Note:	Central 1 does not require a copy of the signed Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007.
Note:	If the business member has a third party generate AFT Transactions on its behalf, it should enter into a separate agreement with the third party. The cash management PAD agreement only addresses the AFT arrangement between the financial institution and the business member.
(Optional) Complete the Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member, Form 3701, for additional coverage
â This agreement highlights the use of
PaymentStream
AFT software and the obligations of business members that use the software. It also provides additional terms for protection and liability. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member, Form 3701
Complete the Application for AFT Service and User Request Forms
Financial institutions complete the
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329, for all new Originators regardless of which party inputs the transactions, which AFT service is used, or what type of AFT Transactions are generated. Complete the form and send it to Central 1 as directed on the form. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: PaymentStream AFT Service Application, Form 5329
Financial institutions set up and edit their internal users and their business membersâ users. See the User Management Part for procedures on setting up users and defining user permissions on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Advise the Business Member of CPA Rule H1 Requirements
â The financial institution must advise the business member Originator (Payee) of CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits (PADs), requirements. This includes the following:
â
Ensuring the Payee signs a Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904, or a Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007
â
Providing the Payee with full information on CPA Rule H1 when the Payee and the financial institution sign a Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904, or a Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007. The financial institution must also advise the Payee of any material amendments to CPA Rule H1.
â
Ensuring that the Payee uses a CPA compliant version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement
Note:	If the financial institution allows the Payee to use a version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement other than Form 1696, the financial institution must review the Payeeâs version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement for CPA compliance.
â
Reviewing and approving the Payeeâs procedures for verifying a Payorâs identity
â
Advising the Payee to act on any Notice of Change information that the financial institution provides
Advise the Payee to go to the Payments Canada website (
www.payments.ca
) to view the following:
â
CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits (PADs)
â
FAQs for PADs (there are FAQs for Payors and FAQs for Payees)
â
The
Business Guide to the Power of Pre-Authorized Debit
, in FAQs for PADs
Establish AFT reports access for the Originator
â All business member Originators download their AFT settlement reports from
PaymentStream
AFT. If the financial institution inputs transactions on behalf of a business member Originator, the parties decide how and when the AFT settlement reports will be provided to the member.
In
PaymentStream
AFT, a CPS Admin Security Officer will communicate the user ID and a temporary password to new users in a secure manner. See the User Management Part for procedures on setting up users and defining user permissions on
PaymentStream
AFT.
In
PaymentStream
AFT, originators can test their file directly on the application to confirm the validity of the AFT file. The system automatically validates a test file when it is uploaded and provides error messages, warnings, or notices to help the user rectify any issues. For procedures on uploading a test file in
PaymentStream
AFT, see the
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for File Upload, Form 5600.
Additional Originator IDs for Business Members
Some business members may request to have multiple AFT Originator IDs set up that are associated with the same business member company. In this instance, the settlement reports for all associated Originator IDs are combined in one report folder for the business members to access through
PaymentStream
AFT. There is a reduced implementation fee for additional Originator IDs since the business member company, their access, and their report folder have already been set up.
Related Information
Payorâs PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans,
Form 1904
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs,
Form 2007
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member,
Form 3701
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application,
Form 5329
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for File Upload,
Form 5600
5.6	Obtaining Payorâs PAD Agreements
The Payee, regardless of whether it is a financial institution Originator or a business member Originator, must obtain the Payorâs authorization to debit their account. The Payee must obtain a Payorâs PAD Agreement from the Payor for Business PADs, Funds Transfer PADs, and Personal PADs. A Payorâs PAD Agreement is not required for Cash Management PADs.
A Payorâs PAD Agreement must include all the mandatory components of a Payorâs PAD Agreement as described in CPA Rule H1, Pre-authorized Debits. The agreement can be authorized by phone, email, internet, or other means.
The Payee must verify that the Payorâs (customerâs) personal and banking information belongs to that Payor, using a commercially reasonable verification method. This can include, but is not limited to, asking the Payor to provide the following:
Numerous forms of identifying information, and then confirming that information from customer databases
Responses to specific questions about information acquired from an internal database or the credit bureau
Verification of a specific piece of information that was sent to an independently verified address either online or off-line
If your business member is the Originator (Payee), your business member must obtain the Payorâs PAD Agreement from its customers. If your financial institution is the Payee (e.g., mortgage or loan payments), your financial institution must obtain a Payorâs PAD Agreement from your members.
It is recommended that all Payees use the Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696. This form is CPA compliant and can be used for all PAD scenarios, including the following:
Various PAD types (Business PADs, Funds Transfer PADs, and Personal PADs)
Set Interval PADs or Sporadic PADs
Fixed amount PADs or variable amount PADs
If your financial institution chooses to use its own version of the Payorâs PAD Agreement, your legal counsel must ensure that the form is CPA compliant. Your financial institution is obligated under CPA Rule H1 to review your business membersâ Payorâs PAD Agreements for CPA compliance. The agreement must include all the mandatory components of a Payorâs PAD Agreement as described in CPA Rule H1. You must also review the Payeeâs procedures for verifying a Payorâs identity, ensuring that the Payee uses a commercially reasonable method that you consent to.
The Payee must provide written confirmation to the Payor at least ten calendar days before the first Pre-authorized Debit (PAD). The Payee and Payor can mutually agree to reduce this period to a minimum of three calendar days if the identity verification method used information known only to the Payor and Payee. See Rule H1 for more information.
Complete the Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696, as follows. See Figure 10 and Figure 11.
â
Payee/Payor Information
â Record the name and contact information of the Payee and the Payor.
â¡
Payment Details
â
Payor Account
â Record the Branch Number and Institution Number of the Payorâs financial institution (Processing Institution) and record the Payorâs account number.
â
Payor Financial Institution Name and Address
â Record the name and address of the Payorâs financial institution.
â
Due Date(s)
â Record the Due Date of the PAD. There may be multiple Due Dates.
â
Amount of Payment
â Select the currency of the PAD and indicate whether the PAD is for a fixed amount or a variable amount. If the amount is fixed, record the amount. If the amount is variable, the Payor can record a maximum amount.
â
Frequency
â Indicate the frequency of the PAD. If the PAD is issued at regular intervals (e.g., weekly, bi-weekly, monthly, annually) it is considered a Set Interval PAD. If the PAD is not issued at regular periods or upon the occurrence of such criteria and/or event(s) as set out in a Payorâs PAD Agreement, the PAD is considered sporadic.
Important
:	The Payee is required to obtain authorization for each Sporadic PAD. The Payor cannot waive this authorization.
â
Payment Type
â Select
Personal PAD
,
Business PAD
, or
Funds Transfer PAD
. See Section 2.1, Definitions, for a definition of each PAD type.

CPA Transaction Type Code
â Record the 3-digit transaction code as provided in CPA Standard 007, Standards for the Use of Transaction Codes and Return Reason Codes in AFT Files. See the Payments Canada website for details (
www.payments.ca
).
Note:	Ensure that the correct CPA transaction code is used when creating AFT Transactions. The transaction code identifies the PAD type and can affect eligibility and time frames for reimbursement claims.
â
Description of PAD (optional)
â Record a description of the PAD as provided in CPA Standard 007, Standards for the Use of Transaction Codes and Return Reason Codes in AFT Files.
â
Payee Account
â Enter the Payeeâs account information if known.
â¢
Authorization
â Ensure that the Payor signs the agreement. If two or more signers on the account are required, both or all Payors must sign.
Figure 10: Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696 Page 1
â£
Waiver of Pre-notification and Confirmation
â The Payor must sign the waiver if pre-notification is not required. If this section is not signed, the Payee must provide the Payor ten calendar daysâ notice before the first PAD and each time there is a change in the amount or payment date of a fixed amount PAD. For variable amount PADs, the Payee must provide the Payor ten calendar daysâ notice before the Due Date of every instance of a variable amount PAD.
Note: 	Pre-notification can only be waived for Set Interval PADs. The Payee must obtain authorization for every Sporadic PAD, which means that the Payor will be notified of every Sporadic PAD.

Cancel Payment
â The Payee can insert the number of daysâ notice required for the Payor to cancel a PAD. This cannot exceed 30 days.
Note: 	The number of days for cancellation notification can be completed in the Cancel Payment section so that the Payor is aware of how much notice they must provide, based on requirement by the Payee. The actual cancellation date (and signature) would only be completed when the payor requests to cancel the PAD.
Retain the original Payorâs PAD Agreement for at least 12 months after it is terminated. Other legal requirements for document retention can also apply. Provide the Payor with a copy of the agreement.
â¥
Terms and Conditions #15
â Enter the number of days within which the Payee must provide written notice to terminate the agreement.
â¦
Terms and Conditions #18
â Specify the governing law and jurisdiction for resolution of claims (e.g., Alberta and City of Calgary).
Note: 	Your financial institution can populate these fields based on your jurisdiction and location (e.g., British Columbia and City of Vancouver). Your financial institution can decide how theyâd like to approach this clause, but ultimately, almost every legal agreement will specify the governing law and jurisdiction for resolution of claims. If this is not specified, it creates lack of certainty in the contract in terms of the laws that apply and also where a plaintiff may bring an action in the event of a dispute. For example, if a plaintiff can convince a court in Texas that there is a real and substantial connection to Texas, then potentially the Texas court could take jurisdiction over the case and potentially apply the laws of another jurisdiction other than Canada.

5.7	Enrolling in AFT Service for Direct Deposits â Business Members
Proceed as follows to set up a business member Originator (Payor) with AFT Service for Direct Deposits:
Complete the Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043
â The Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043, is an agreement between the sponsoring financial institution and the business member and pertains to the processing of Direct Deposits. If the business member contracts a third-party to generate AFT Transactions on its behalf, the business member can draft a separate agreement. The Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043, only addresses the AFT arrangement between the financial institution and the business member.
The Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043, is not required by Payments Canada, but is required by the Deposit and Loan Agreement that each financial institution signed with Central 1. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043
(Optional) Complete the Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member, Form 3701, for additional coverage
â
This agreement highlights the use of
PaymentStream
AFT software and the obligations of business members that use the software. It also provides additional terms for protection and liability. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Automated Funds Transfer Service Agreement â Business Member, Form 3701
Complete the Application for AFT Service
Financial institutions complete the
PaymentStream
AFT Service Application, Form 5329, for all new Originators regardless of which party inputs the transactions, which AFT service is used, or what type of AFT Transactions are generated. Complete the form and send it to Central 1 as directed on the form. See Figure 14. Completion of the form is self-explanatory.
Figure 14: PaymentStream AFT Service Application, Form 5329
Financial institutions set up and edit their own users and their business membersâ users. See the User Management Part for procedures on setting up users and defining user permissions on
PaymentStream
AFT.
Discuss the Direct Deposit Application and voided cheques with the business member
â The business member (Payor) can obtain a Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000, and a voided cheque from each Payee to confirm account information. This is not a CPA requirement although Payments Canada strongly recommends that the Payee provide a voided cheque on the account to be credited. This ensures that the Payor receives complete and accurate account information.
The Payor (business member) can alter the Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000, as required. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000
Establish AFT reports access for the Originator
â All business member Originators download their AFT reports from
PaymentStream
AFT. If the financial institution inputs transactions on behalf of a business member Originator, the parties decide how and when the AFT reports will be provided to the member.
In
PaymentStream
AFT, the Client Centre user ID is the only ID required to access the system. A CPS Admin Security Officer will communicate the Client Centre user ID and a temporary password to new Client Centre users in a secure manner.
In
PaymentStream
AFT, Originators can test their file directly on the application to confirm validity of the AFT files. The system automatically validates a test file when it is uploaded and provides error messages, warnings, or notices to help the user rectify any issues. For procedures on uploading a test file in
PaymentStream
AFT, see the
PaymentStream
AFT Guide for File Upload, Form 5600.
Additional Originator IDs for Business Members
Some business members may request to have multiple AFT Originator IDs set up that are associated with the same business member company. In this instance, the settlement reports for all associated Originator IDs are combined in one report folder for the business members to access through
PaymentStream
AFT. There is a reduced implementation fee for additional Originator IDs since the business member company, their access, and their report folder have already been set up.

5.8	Enrolling in AFT Service â Non-Business Members
If a non-business member wants to transfer funds from their financial institution account to an account at another financial institution, the financial institution can set up Direct Deposits from the memberâs account. The Payee may or may not be the same individual as the Payor (the member).
There is some risk in generating Direct Deposits for members. Specifically, insufficient funds in a memberâs account can result in an overdraft when the Offsetting Entry for a Direct Deposit transaction is posted. See Section 4.5, Policy for Mitigating Risk and Liability, for more information.
Note:	The financial institution can recommend that the member use
Interac
e-Transfers
Â®
as an alternate means of transferring funds to another account.
If the financial institution chooses to originate Direct Deposits for the member, the financial institution must create the transactions on the memberâs behalf. The financial institution should already have an Originator ID set up to create AFT Transactions for individual members.
Proceed as follows to set up Direct Deposits for a non-business member:
Obtain the Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Member Payors, Form 5966, from the member (Payor). Although this form is not mandatory, it provides the memberâs authorization to debit their account and the Payeeâs account information and authorization for Direct Deposits. While the CPA does not require authorization from the Payee for Direct Deposits, the financial institution can choose to obtain it. If the member is both the Payor and the Payee, the member will complete the form in full. If the Payee is an individual other than the member, the financial institution can request that the Payee complete the Payee Information and Payee Banking Information sections. See Figure 16.
Note:	The financial institution can alter the Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Member Payors, Form 5966, as required.
Figure 16: Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Member Payors, Form 5966
Obtain a voided cheque from the Payee. The CPA strongly recommends that the Payee provide a voided cheque on the account to be credited. This ensures that the financial institution receives complete and accurate account information.
Related Information
Direct Deposit Application â Non-business Member Payors,
Form 5966
5.9	AFT Transaction and Offsetting Entry Options
One-to-One Transactions
These are transactions typically generated on behalf of an individual member. For example, a member with a mortgage at the financial institution can request that the financial institution debit their account at another financial institution and then credit the mortgage account. This creates one debit and one credit. In other words, this is a âone-to-oneâ transaction. Although it is uncommon, business members can also create this type of transaction.
The financial institution can be the Payor financial institution (where the memberâs account is debited) or the Payee financial institution (where the memberâs account is credited). The second party can have an account at any other Canadian financial institution.
Many-to-One Transactions
These are transactions typically generated by a business member or by the financial institution on behalf of a business member. For example, a business member that offers Direct Deposit payroll services to its employees creates multiple credits for one debit. âMany-to-oneâ transactions are those in which there are many credits for one debit (as above) or many debits for one credit.
In âmany-to-oneâ transactions, the party for the one transaction (the employer in the above example) is the financial institutionâs business member. The other parties may have accounts at any Canadian financial institution.
Many-to-Many Transactions
Occasionally, business members create an AFT file containing multiple transactions where each has its own Offsetting Transaction. These are known as âmany-to-manyâ transactions.
5.10	Changing File Limits for AFT Service via FTP
AFT Originators are subject to originator limits. In
PaymentStream
AFT, financial institutions manage their own limits and the limits for their business members. See the Originating AFT via
PaymentStream
AFT Part for more information.
Originators that upload files to Central 1âs File and Report Exchange, proceed as follows to change the file limit(s) for an Originator ID:
Note:	This form is for BC and Atlantic regions only.
Complete the File Limit Change Request AFT Service via FTP, Form 1641, and indicate the new limit(s). Complete one form per Originator ID. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: File Limit Change Request AFT Service via FTP, Form 1641
Authorized financial institution Signing Officers must sign the form.
Forward the request to Central 1 as indicated on the form. Requests are processed the day they are received.

Form 1641
5.11	Financial Institution Transit Information
To create AFT Transactions, the Originator requires the transit information of the Receiving Financial Institution. The Canadian Payments Association (CPA) maintains a list of the Transit Numbers and addresses of all financial institutions in Canada. This list is known as the Financial Institutions File (FIF).
The FIF is available via CD-ROM or by downloading through the CPAâs File Transfer Protocol System (FTP). Contact Central 1âs Manager Payments Reconciliation and Financial Administration for information on obtaining the FIF. See Section 1.6, Contacts and Websites, for details. Once the FIF is obtained, updates to the file will be received independently of Central 1. There is a cost associated with acquiring the FIF.
